Title: Big Bang
Description: hypothetical begin of the Universe through expansion out of an infinitely small and infinitely dense state

Title: Formation of Earth
Description: Gravity pulls swirling gas and dust into the third planet from the sun. Earth begins as a molten inferno, eventually cooling to form the solid crust, atmosphere, and vast oceans that would eventually cradle life.

Title: The Spark of Life
Description: Deep in fossilized hydrothermal vents, simple prokaryotes begin a multibillion-year journey. This transition from chemistry to biology marks the most profound shift in the history of the planet.

Title: The Age of Dinosaurs
Description: From the Triassic to the Cretaceous, dinosaurs were the undisputed masters of Earth. Their vast reign ended in fire and dust with the Chicxulub asteroid impact, clearing the path for the rise of mammals.

Title: Rise of Homo Sapiens
column8: Equipped with cognitive capacity for language, art, and complex tools, Homo sapiens begin migrating across the planet, eventually outlasting all other hominid species through innovation and social structure.

Title: Agricultural Revolution
column8: In the Fertile Crescent, humans begin domesticating plants and animals. This Neolithic shift leads to permanent settlements, surplus food, and the eventual rise of specialized labor and social hierarchies.

Title: astronomy
Description: natural science studying celestial objects and phenomena in the cosmos

Title: Empire of the Nile
Description: Pharaoh Narmer unites the Nile Valley, initiating a civilization that would build pyramids, develop hieroglyphics, and endure for three millennia.

Title: Pythagoras
Description: Greek philosopher and mathematician (c. 570 – c. 495 BC)

Title: Laozi
Description: 6th-century BC semi-legendary Chinese philosopher, founder of Taoism

Title: The Buddha
Description: Indian philosopher and the founder of Buddhism (623 or 563 BCE – 543 or 483 BCE)

Title: Aristotle
Description: 4th-century BCE Classical Greek philosopher and polymath

Title: Alexander the Great
Description: king of Macedonia and conqueror of Achaemenid Persia (356–323 BC)

Title: Archimedes
Description: Greek mathematician and physicist (c. 287 – c. 212 BC)

Title: Cicero
Description: Roman statesman, lawyer, orator, and philosopher (106–43 BC)

Title: Augustus
Description: first emperor of the Roman Empire and founder of the Julio-Claudian dynasty

Title: The Life of Jesus of Nazareth
Description: Regardless of belief, the life and teachings of Jesus of Nazareth fundamentally reshaped the Western world

Title: Justin Martyr
Description: 2nd century CE Christian apologist and martyr

Title: Discovery of Gunpowder
Description: Taoist alchemists in China mixed saltpeter, sulfur, and charcoal while seeking an elixir of life. The resulting explosive changed the nature of global conflict and the power of empires.

Title: Thomas Aquinas
Description: Italian Dominican friar, philosopher, Catholic priest, and Doctor of the Church (1225–1274)

Title: Renaissance
Description: cultural movement that spanned the period roughly from the 14th to the 17th century

Title: Niccolò Machiavelli
Description: Italian diplomat and political and military theorist (1469–1527)

Title: Albrecht Dürer
Description: German painter, printmaker, mathematician, and theorist (1471–1528)

Title: Treaty of Tordesillas
Description: treaty dividing territory between Portugal and Spain

Title: Blaise Pascal
Description: French mathematician, physicist, inventor, writer, and Christian philosopher (1623–1662)

Title: Louis XIV of France
Description: King of France and of Navarre from 1643 to 1715 (1638–1715)

Title: Innocent XIII
Description: pope of the Catholic Church from 1721 to 1724

Title: Adam Smith
Description: Scottish moral philosopher and political economist (1723–1790)

Title: George Washington
Description: Founding Father and first U.S. president; soldier and slave owner (1732–1799)

Title: John Adams
Description: Founding Father, U.S. president from 1797 to 1801

Title: Thomas Jefferson
Description: president of the United States from 1801 to 1809 (1743–1826)

Title: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
Description: German writer, artist, natural scientist and politician (1749–1832)

Title: Friedrich Schiller
Description: German playwright, poet, philosopher and historian (1759–1805)

Title: Napoleon
Description: French military leader, French Emperor 1804–1814 and again in 1815 (1769–1821)

Title: Declaration of Independence
column8: Drafted primarily by Thomas Jefferson, the declaration stated the philosophical and practical reasons for the American Revolution, asserting that

Title: U.S. Constitution Signed
column8: The framers created a system of checks and balances that became a blueprint for modern representative democracy.

Title: Victor Hugo
Description: French novelist, poet, dramatist and politician (1802–1885)

Title: Abraham Lincoln
Description: president of the United States from 1861 to 1865 (1809–1865)

Title: Woodrow Wilson
Description: president of the United States from 1913 to 1921 (1856–1924)

Title: Theodore Roosevelt
Description: 26th President of the United States from 1901 to 1909 (1858–1919)

Title: Alaska Purchase
Description: 1867 sale of Alaska to the USA by Russia

Title: Vladimir Lenin
Description: founding leader of the Soviet Union from 1922 to 1924 (1870–1924)

Title: Joseph Stalin
Description: leader of the Soviet Union from 1924 to 1953

Title: Mustafa Kemal Atatürk
Description: founding father of the Republic of Turkey (1881–1938)

Title: Harry S. Truman
Description: president of the United States from 1945 to 1953; politician (1884–1972)

Title: Berne Convention for the Protection of Literary and Artistic Works
Description: 1880s international copyright treaty adopted by 160+ countries

Title: Adolf Hitler
Description: dictator of Germany from 1933 to 1945, main instigator of World War II and leader of the Holocaust (1889–1945)

Title: Jawaharlal Nehru
Description: Prime Minister of India from 1947 to 1964

Title: Dwight D. Eisenhower
Description: president of the United States from 1953 to 1961 (1890–1969)

Title: Mao Zedong
Description: 1st chairman of the Chinese Communist Party and founder of the People

Title: Boxer Rebellion
Description: anti-imperialist uprising which took place in China 1899-1901

Title: Triple Entente
Description: early 20th century alliance between France, Russia and the United Kingdom

Title: Simone de Beauvoir
Description: French philosopher, social theorist and activist (1908–1986)

Title: Ronald Reagan
Description: president of the United States from 1981 to 1989 and actor (1911–2004)

Title: Richard Nixon
Description: president of the United States from 1969 to 1974 (1913–1994)

Title: The World Wars
Description: Two global conflicts that reshaped borders, technology, and international law. WWI saw the end of old empires, while WWII ended in the nuclear age and the formation of the United Nations.

Title: Treaty of London
Description: secret pact between the Triple Entente and Italy, signed in London on 26 April 1915

Title: Sykes–Picot Agreement
Description: secret agreement signed in 1916 between France and the United Kingdom providing for the division of the Middle East at the end of the First World War

Title: John F. Kennedy
Description: president of the United States from 1961 to 1963 (1917–1963)

Title: Nelson Mandela
Description: First President of South Africa and anti-apartheid activist (1918–2013)

Title: Jimmy Carter
Description: president of the United States from 1977 to 1981 (1924–2024)

Title: George H. W. Bush
Description: president of the United States from 1989 to 1993 (1924–2018)

Title: Benedict XVI
Description: 265 th pope of the Catholic Church (2005–2013)

Title: Martin Luther King Jr.
Description: American Baptist minister and civil rights leader (1929–1968)

Title: Mikhail Gorbachev
Description: leader of the Soviet Union from 1985 to 1991 (1931–2022)

Title: Yuri Gagarin
Description: Soviet pilot and cosmonaut, first human in space (1934–1968)

Title: Anti-Comintern Pact
Description: pact between Nazi Germany and Japan prior to World War II

Title: Munich agreement
Description: 1938 cession of German-speaking Czechoslovakia to the German Reich

Title: Phoney War
Description: eight-month period at the start of World War II, during which there was only one limited military land operation on the Western Front

Title: Tripartite Pact
Description: treaty establishing the Axis Powers of World War Two

Title: Potsdam Conference
Description: meeting of the Allied heads of state near the end of World War II

Title: Donald Trump
Description: President of the United States (2017–2021; since 2025)

Title: George W. Bush
Description: President of the United States from 2001 to 2009

Title: Bill Clinton
Description: President of the United States from 1993 to 2001 (born 1946)

Title: General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade
Vladimir Putin
Description: 2nd and 4th President of Russia (2000–2008, 2012–present), 7th and 11th Prime Minister of Russia (1999–2000, 2008–2012), Director of the Federal Security Service (1998–1999) and Deputy Mayor of Saint Petersburg (1994–1996)

Title: Xi Jinping
Description: General Secretary of the Chinese Communist Party since 2012 (born 1953)

Title: Nicolas Sarkozy
Description: 23rd president of the French Republic from 2007 to 2012

Title: Barack Obama
Description: president of the United States from 2009 to 2017 (born 1961)

Title: Kamala Harris
Description: Vice President of the United States from 2021 to 2025

Title: Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons
Helsinki Accords
Description: 1975 non-binding European and North American political agreement

Title: Emmanuel Macron
Description: president of France and Co-Prince of Andorra since 2017

Title: Schengen Agreement
Description: treaty which led to the creation of Europe

Title: German reunification
Description: process in 1990 in which the German Democratic Republic became part of the Federal Republic of Germany

Title: European Charter for Regional or Minority Languages
Rome Statute of the International Criminal Court
Description: 1998 international treaty establishing the International Criminal Court

Title: Paris Agreement
Description: international agreement from 12 December 2015 within the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) dealing with greenhouse gas emissions mitigation, adaptation and finance starting in the year 2020
